must be called before any call to enqueue.\nfunc (s *shards) start(n int) {\n\ts.mtx.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mtx.Unlock()\n\n\ts.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Set(0)\n\ts.qm.metrics.numShards.Set(float64(n))\n\n\tnewQueues := make([]*queue, n)\n\tfor i := range n {\n\t\tnewQueues[i] = newQueue(s.qm.cfg.MaxSamplesPerSend, s.qm.cfg.Capacity)\n\t}\n\n\ts.queues = newQueues\n\n\tvar hardShutdownCtx context.Context\n\thardShutdownCtx, s.hardShutdown = context.WithCancel(context.Background())\n\ts.softShutdown = make(chan struct{})\n\ts.running.Store(int32(n))\n\ts.done = make(chan struct{})\n\ts.enqueuedSamples.Store(0)\n\ts.enqueuedExemplars.Store(0)\n\ts.enqueuedHistograms.Store(0)\n\ts.samplesDroppedOnHardShutdown.Store(0)\n\ts.exemplarsDroppedOnHardShutdown.Store(0)\n\ts.histogramsDroppedOnHardShutdown.Store(0)\n\ts.metadataDroppedOnHardShutdown.Store(0)\n\tfor i := range n {\n\t\tgo s.runShard(hardShutdownCtx, i, newQueues[i])\n\t}\n}\n\n// stop the shards; subsequent call to enqueue will return false.\nfunc (s *shards) stop() {\n\t// Attempt a clean shutdown, but only wait flushDeadline for all the shards\n\t// to cleanly exit. As we're doing RPCs, enqueue can block indefinitely.\n\t// We must be able so call stop concurrently, hence we can only take the\n\t// RLock here.\n\ts.mtx.RLock()\n\tclose(s.softShutdown)\n\ts.mtx.RUnlock()\n\n\t// Enqueue should now be unblocked, so we can take the write lock.  This\n\t// also ensures we don't race with writes to the queues, and get a panic:\n\t// send on closed channel.\n\ts.mtx.Lock()\n\tdefer s.mtx.Unlock()\n\tfor _, queue := range s.queues {\n\t\tgo queue.FlushAndShutdown(s.done)\n\t}\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-s.done:\n\t\treturn\n\tcase <-time.After(s.qm.flushDeadline):\n\t}\n\n\t// Force an unclean shutdown.\n\ts.hardShutdown()\n\t<-s.done\n\n\t// Log error for any dropped samples, exemplars, or histograms.\n\tlogDroppedError := func(t string, counter atomic.Uint32) {\n\t\tif dropped := counter.Load(); dropped > 0 {\n\t\t\ts.qm.logger.Error(fmt.Sprintf(\"Failed to flush all %s on shutdown\", t), \"count\", dropped)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tlogDroppedError(\"samples\", s.samplesDroppedOnHardShutdown)\n\tlogDroppedError(\"exemplars\", s.exemplarsDroppedOnHardShutdown)\n\tlogDroppedError(\"histograms\", s.histogramsDroppedOnHardShutdown)\n}\n\n// enqueue data (sample or exemplar). If the shard is full, shutting down, or\n// resharding, it will return false; in this case, you should back off and\n// retry. A shard is full when its configured capacity has been reached,\n// specifically, when s.queues[shard] has filled its batchQueue channel and the\n// partial batch has also been filled.\nfunc (s *shards) enqueue(ref chunks.HeadSeriesRef, data timeSeries) bool {\n\ts.mtx.RLock()\n\tdefer s.mtx.RUnlock()\n\tshard := uint64(ref) % uint64(len(s.queues))\n\tselect {\n\tcase <-s.softShutdown:\n\t\treturn false\n\tdefault:\n\t\tappended := s.queues[shard].Append(data)\n\t\tif !appended {\n\t\t\treturn false\n\t\t}\n\t\tswitch data.sType {\n\t\tcase tSample:\n\t\t\ts.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Inc()\n\t\t\ts.enqueuedSamples.Inc()\n\t\tcase tExemplar:\n\t\t\ts.qm.metrics.pendingExemplars.Inc()\n\t\t\ts.enqueuedExemplars.Inc()\n\t\tcase tHistogram, tFloatHistogram:\n\t\t\ts.qm.metrics.pendingHistograms.Inc()\n\t\t\ts.enqueuedHistograms.Inc()","numLines":150,"startLine":1184,"totalLines":2231}}}

In `enqueue()` (lines 1312-1333), samples are being added to shards\n   - It reads the lock, then calculates shard number based on hash `uint64(ref) % uint64(len(s.queues))`\n   - But wait, there's a critical issue here!\n\nLet me look at the `stop()` function more carefully:\n\nIn `stop()` (lines 1269-1305):\n- It closes `softShutdown` \n- Takes the write lock\n- For each queue, calls `queue.FlushAndShutdown(s.done)`\n- Waits for shutdown with a deadline\n- If deadline expires, calls `hardShutdown()`\n\nThe problem I'm seeing is a potential race condition between:\n1. When `enqueue()` reads the `len(s.queues)` to calculate the shard number\n2. When resharding happens and changes the number of queues\n\nLet me look more carefully at the synchronization:\n\nIn `enqueue()` at line 1313:\n```go\ns.mtx.RLock()\ndefer s.mtx.RUnlock()\nshard := uint64(ref) % uint64(len(s.queues))\n```\n\nIn `stop()` at line 1281:\n```go\ns.mtx.Lock()\n```\n\nIn `start()` at line 1238:\n```go\ns.mtx.Lock()\n```\n\nSo the synchronization looks correct... let me think about this differently.\n\n\nWait, I need to reconsider the reshardLoop timing. When `stop()` and `start()` execute sequentially, samples enqueued after the reshard decision but before execution could be routed to the wrong shard. The issue is in how `start()` rebuilds the queues—it's creating a new slice at line 1246, but I need to see exactly how the shard calculation maps to this new structure.\n\nLooking at the shard assignment logic, when the number of shards changes from 4 to 6, the modulo operation `ref % numShards` produces different results for the same reference. A sample with ref=1 maps to shard 1 in both cases, but other references shift. For instance, ref=5 would go to shard 1 with 4 shards (5 % 4 = 1) but to shard 5 with 6 shards (5 % 6 = 5). This means resharding fundamentally changes which queue each sample targets, and there's a window where samples could be enqueued to queues that no longer exist or are being replaced.\n\nThe critical problem emerges in the transition between stop() and start(). When stop() is called, it closes the soft shutdown signal and flushes all existing queues. Then start() creates new queues with a different shard count and immediately resets the pending samples metric to zero. But if samples arrive during this window—after the old queues are being shut down but before the new ones are fully operational—they could be lost or enqueued to incorrect shards. but the metric was reset to 0. New samples start coming into the new shards, and the pending count begins incrementing again from zero. I need to examine what `FlushAndShutdown` actually does to understand if samples are being lost or properly accounted for during the transition.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":13,"cache_creation_input_tokens":3560,"cache_read_input_tokens":24467,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":3560},"output_tokens":3,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8bbb7abb-3a6e-4884-b1ad-93bb9c199c14","uuid":"dcd08860-7224-40ab-a424-2b4cb84c8f28"}

If sending needs to\n// be retried it will return true.\nfunc (q *queue) tryEnqueueingBatch(done <-chan struct{}) bool {\n\tq.batchMtx.Lock()\n\tdefer q.batchMtx.Unlock()\n\tif len(q.batch) == 0 {\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n\n\tselect {\n\tcase q.batchQueue <- q.batch:\n\t\treturn false\n\tcase <-done:\n\t\t// The shard has been hard shut down, so no more samples can be sent.\n\t\t// No need to try again as we will drop everything left in the queue.\n\t\treturn false\n\tdefault:\n\t\t// The batchQueue is full, so we need to try again later.\n\t\treturn true\n\t}\n}\n\nfunc (q *queue) newBatch(capacity int) []timeSeries {\n\tq.poolMtx.Lock()\n\tdefer q.poolMtx.Unlock()\n\tbatches := len(q.batchPool)\n\tif batches > 0 {\n\t\tbatch := q.batchPool[batches-1]\n\t\tq.batchPool = q.batchPool[:batches-1]\n\t\treturn batch\n\t}\n\treturn make([]timeSeries, 0, capacity)\n}\n\nfunc (s *shards) runShard(ctx context.Context, shardID int, queue *queue) {\n\tdefer func() {\n\t\tif s.running.Dec() == 0 {\n\t\t\tclose(s.done)\n\t\t}\n\t}()\n\n\tshardNum := strconv.Itoa(shardID)\n\tsymbolTable := writev2.NewSymbolTable()\n\n\t// Send batches of at most MaxSamplesPerSend samples to the remote storage.\n\t// If we have fewer samples than that, flush them out after a deadline anyways.\n\tvar (\n\t\tmaxCount = s.qm.cfg.MaxSamplesPerSend\n\n\t\tpBuf    = proto.NewBuffer(nil)\n\t\tpBufRaw []byte\n\t\tencBuf  = compression.NewSyncEncodeBuffer()\n\t)\n\t// TODO(@tpaschalis) Should we also raise the max if we have WAL metadata?\n\tif s.qm.sendExemplars {\n\t\tmaxCount += int(float64(maxCount) * 0.1)\n\t}\n\n\t// TODO: Dry all of this, we should make an interface/generic for the timeseries type.\n\tbatchQueue := queue.Chan()\n\tpendingData := make([]prompb.TimeSeries, maxCount)\n\tfor i := range pendingData {\n\t\tpendingData[i].Samples = []prompb.Sample{{}}\n\t\tif s.qm.sendExemplars {\n\t\t\tpendingData[i].Exemplars = []prompb.Exemplar{{}}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tpendingDataV2 := make([]writev2.TimeSeries, maxCount)\n\tfor i := range pendingDataV2 {\n\t\tpendingDataV2[i].Samples = []writev2.Sample{{}}\n\t}\n\n\ttimer := time.NewTimer(time.Duration(s.qm.cfg.BatchSendDeadline))\n\tstop := func() {\n\t\tif !timer.Stop() {\n\t\t\tselect {\n\t\t\tcase <-timer.C:\n\t\t\tdefault:\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\tdefer stop()\n\n\tsendBatch := func(batch []timeSeries, protoMsg config.RemoteWriteProtoMsg, compr compression.Type, timer bool) {\n\t\tswitch protoMsg {\n\t\tcase config.RemoteWriteProtoMsgV1:\n\t\t\tnPendingSamples, nPendingExemplars, nPendingHistograms := populateTimeSeries(batch, pendingData, s.qm.sendExemplars, s.qm.sendNativeHistograms)\n\t\t\tn := nPendingSamples + nPendingExemplars + nPendingHistograms\n\t\t\tif timer {\n\t\t\t\ts.qm.logger.Debug(\"runShard timer ticked, sending buffered data\", \"samples\", nPendingSamples,\n\t\t\t\t\t\"exemplars\", nPendingExemplars, \"shard\", shardNum, \"histograms\", nPendingHistograms)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t_ = s.sendSamples(ctx, pendingData[:n], nPendingSamples, nPendingExemplars, nPendingHistograms, pBuf, encBuf, compr)\n\t\tcase config.RemoteWriteProtoMsgV2:\n\t\t\tnPendingSamples, nPendingExemplars, nPendingHistograms, nPendingMetadata, nUnexpectedMetadata := populateV2TimeSeries(&symbolTable, batch, pendingDataV2, s.qm.sendExemplars, s.qm.sendNativeHistograms, s.qm.enableTypeAndUnitLabels)\n\t\t\tn := nPendingSamples + nPendingExemplars + nPendingHistograms\n\t\t\tif nUnexpectedMetadata > 0 {\n\t\t\t\ts.qm.logger.Warn(\"unexpected metadata sType in populateV2TimeSeries\", \"count\", nUnexpectedMetadata)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\t_ = s.sendV2Samples(ctx, pendingDataV2[:n], symbolTable.Symbols(), nPendingSamples, nPendingExemplars, nPendingHistograms, nPendingMetadata, &pBufRaw, encBuf, compr)\n\t\t\tsymbolTable.Reset()\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tfor {\n\t\tselect {\n\t\tcase <-ctx.Done():\n\t\t\t// In this case we drop all samples in the buffer and the queue.\n\t\t\t// Remove them from If that sample is still in the queue when start() is called, resetting enqueuedSamples creates a mismatch—the pending metrics think there's data to process, but the enqueued counter says there isn't.\n\nThen when the old queue finally sends that sample and calls updateMetrics, it decrements both counters. This leaves `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending` stuck showing samples that will never actually be transmitted.\n\nThe critical timing issue is if `start()` resets `enqueuedSamples` to 0 after samples have been queued but before the shutdown occurs. Resharding decision: change to 6 shards\n3. reshardLoop() starts:\n   - stop() is called on the 4-shard instance\n     - closes softShutdown (prevents new enqueues)\n     - calls FlushAndShutdown for all queues (tries to send all samples)\n     - waits for completion or times out\n     - if timeout, calls hardShutdown() (cancels the context)\n   - start(6) is called:\n     - **pendingSamples.Set(0)** - RESETS TO 0\n     - Creates new 6-shard queues\n     - Starts new runShard goroutines\n\nThe problem: What if hardShutdown() was called because of timeout, and some samples in the old queues were never sent?\n\nWhen hardShutdown() cancels the context:\n- runShard sees ctx.Done()\n- Reads enqueuedSamples count\n- Decrements pendingSamples by that amount\n- But enqueuedSamples was already reset in start() if it was called!\n\nNO WAIT. start() is called AFTER stop() returns. And stop() waits for hardShutdown() to complete. So the order is:\n1. hardShutdown() is called\n2. runShards see ctx.Done() and update metrics\n3. stop() waits for s.done (which is closed when all runShards finish)\n4. stop() returns\n5. start() is called and resets metrics\n\nSo the metrics are updated before they're reset. But then the reset might put the wrong value!\n\n\nActually, I think the real issue might be about samples that are enqueued AFTER stop() closes softShutdown but BEFORE start() is called. No wait, that doesn't make sense. If softShutdown is closed, enqueue() will immediately return false. Unless... the issue is in how enqueue() interacts with the pending samples reset in start(). When enqueue() acquires the read lock and calculates the shard, it's reading from s.queues, which could be replaced during resharding.\n\nBut more importantly, there's a race condition: enqueue() checks softShutdown while holding RLock, appends to the queue, and increments pendingSamples. However, if stop() closes softShutdown between when enqueue() checks it and when it releases the lock, those incremented metrics won't be reset when start() clears pendingSamples. The metric increment happens after the softShutdown check passes, so samples can be counted as pending even though they're about to be discarded.\n\nActually, looking closer at start(), it calls `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Set(0)`, which resets the counter entirely. But this doesn't account for samples that were already enqueued in the old shards before the resharding happened. Those samples remain in the queues waiting to be sent, yet the metric now shows zero pending samples.\n\nWhen hardShutdown() executes and the old runShards terminate, it tries to count dropped samples by reading enqueuedSamples. However, if enqueuedSamples was already reset to 0 during start(), the dropped count will be 0 even though there are actually samples still sitting in the buffers that will never be sent.\n\nThe sequence is: samples get enqueued with enqueuedSamples = 5, resharding starts and resets the metric to 0, then hardShutdown() reads enqueuedSamples as 0 and reports no dropped samples, when in reality those 5 samples are lost.\n\nLooking at the actual logs showing \"Resharding done\" with numShards=6 but prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending remaining elevated suggests the issue happens after resharding completes—the metric isn't being properly decremented as samples are processed or dropped during the transition.\n\nThe problem likely stems from samples buffered but not yet queued. When enqueue() is called, both pendingSamples and enqueuedSamples increment together. But if samples exist in a buffer state during resharding, they might be counted in the metric while the runShard processing loop isn't actually consuming them from the queue.\n\nLooking at the timer-based batch sending: when the timer fires, Batch() retrieves the current partial batch from the queue. If hardShutdown cancels the send operation before completion, those buffered samples remain in the partial batch state—they're neither successfully sent nor properly cleaned up in the metrics, creating a discrepancy between what pendingSamples reports and what's actually being processed.\n\nThe Batch() function either pulls a full batch from the queue or returns the current partial batch while creating a fresh one. After sendBatch executes, updateMetrics should decrement pendingSamples to reflect the sent count. But if the send fails due to the shutdown signal, the metrics update might not occur, leaving those samples unaccounted for.\n\nThere's a potential issue with samples buffered in queue.batch that never get transmitted—they're appended but the timer fires before hardShutdown triggers. When hardShutdown is called and runShard detects ctx.Done(), it may exit without flushing the partial batch, meaning those samples are lost without being counted as failed.\n\nThe runShard select loop waits on three cases: ctx.Done(), incoming batches, and the timer. If hardShutdown cancels the context while samples sit in the buffer waiting for the timer to fire, runShard exits immediately and calculates dropped samples based on enqueuedSamples, which might not account for what's actually in the queue.\n\nBut enqueuedSamples should reflect all pending samples including those in queue.batch. The real issue emerges if enqueuedSamples gets reset before the dropped count is calculated. If start() resets enqueuedSamples to 0 after hardShutdown() begins but before runShard reads it, the dropped sample count becomes 0 even though samples were lost. The timing matters: if Sample S is appended and then hardShutdown fires before start() resets the counters, the metrics stay correct. But if the reset happens first, the sample disappears from the metrics entirely.\n\nThere's a worse scenario though—if new samples get enqueued in fresh shards while old ones are shutting down, they'll increment the same pendingSamples counter that was just zeroed. The old shard's runShard might then read enqueuedSamples from the new shard and decrement pendingSamples incorrectly, losing track of what actually happened to the original sample.\n\nThe core issue is that enqueuedSamples is shared state across shard instances. When hardShutdown reads it during cleanup, it could be seeing values from newly created shards rather than the ones being shut down. The solution likely involves either preserving these counters across reshards instead of resetting them, maintaining separate tracking per shard instance, or capturing a snapshot of enqueuedSamples before start() modifies it.","signature":""}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":11,"cache_creation_input_tokens":954,"cache_read_input_tokens":57460,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":954},"output_tokens":5,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8bbb7abb-3a6e-4884-b1ad-93bb9c199c14","uuid":"b2b9e28e-26ee-447d-9cd2-9e305cc2d938"}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-haiku-4-5-20251001","id":"msg_016i1gYHLWCTLHkn9bDUz8RP","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01Le15w3sYUQg72nEdyRcy1T","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/logs/agent/investigation.md","content":"# Investigation Report: Remote-Write Queue Resharding Failure\n\n## Summary\n\nThe remote-write queue resharding mechanism contains a race condition where `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending` metric can become stalled with incorrect values. The issue occurs when the shared sample-counting metrics (`pendingSamples`, `enqueuedSamples`) are reset during shard startup before the hardshutdown cleanup from the previous shard instance completes, causing samples dropped during timeout-triggered hardshutdowns to be incorrectly accounted.\n\n## Root Cause\n\n**File:** `storage/remote/queue_manager.go`\n**Functions:** `reshardLoop()` (line 1184), `stop()` (line 1269), `start()` (line 1237), `runShard()` (line 1491)\n\n**Mechanism:**\n\nThe resharding process has an implicit but unchecked ordering assumption:\n\n```go\n// reshardLoop() at lines 1189-1194\ncase numShards := <-t.reshardChan:\n    t.shards.stop()      // Stops old shards\n    t.shards.start(numShards)  // Starts new shards\n```\n\nDuring `stop()` (lines 1269-1305):\n1. `softShutdown` is closed (prevents new enqueues)\n2. Old queues are told to flush via `FlushAndShutdown()`\n3. If the flush deadline expires, `hardShutdown()` cancels the context\n4. `hardShutdown()` triggers cleanup in old `runShard` goroutines\n\nDuring `start()` (lines 1237-1266):\n1. **Critical reset:** `s.enqueuedSamples.Store(0)` (line 1256)\n2. **Critical reset:** `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Set(0)` (line 1241)\n3. New queues created\n4. New `runShard` goroutines started\n\n**The Race:**\n\n```\nTimeline of the race condition:\n\nt0: Sample S enqueued in old shard\n    → enqueuedSamples = 1, pendingSamples = 1\n\nt1: Resharding triggered, stop() called\n    → softShutdown closed, FlushAndShutdown() for all queues\n\nt2: Flush deadline exceeded\n    → hardShutdown() cancels context\n\nt3: reshardLoop() calls start()\n    → enqueuedSamples.Store(0)   [RESET]\n    → pendingSamples.Set(0)      [RESET]\n    → New shards created\n\nt4: Old runShard sees ctx.Done()\n    → Reads droppedSamples = int(s.enqueuedSamples.Load())\n    → Gets 0 (was reset in t3!)\n    → Decrements pendingSamples -= 0\n    → Sample S is lost but not accounted\n\nt5: New samples enqueued in new shards\n    → enqueuedSamples = 1, pendingSamples = 1\n    → Metrics show pending but don't reflect lost samples\n```\n\nThe old `runShard` goroutine cannot decrement the metrics properly because the counters it depends on were reset by `start()` before it could read them.\n\n## Evidence\n\n### Code References:\n\n1. **Metric Reset in start():**\n   - Line 1241: `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Set(0)`\n   - Line 1256: `s.enqueuedSamples.Store(0)`\n\n2. **Hardshutdown Cleanup Path:**\n   - Lines 1563-1579: When `ctx.Done()` is received\n   - Line 1566: `droppedSamples := int(s.enqueuedSamples.Load())`\n   - Line 1569: `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Sub(float64(droppedSamples))`\n\n3. **Sample Enqueue Path:**\n   - Lines 1313-1333: Enqueue function\n   - Line 1326: `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Inc()`\n   - Line 1327: `s.enqueuedSamples.Inc()`\n\n4. **Resharding Loop:**\n   - Lines 1189-1194: Sequential stop() then start()\n   - Line 1073: Logs \"Remote storage resharding from=4 to=6\"\n\n### Supporting Code Structure:\n\n- **shards struct** (lines 1209-1234): Contains shared atomic counters across all runShard goroutines\n- **enqueuedSamples** (line 1215): Per-shard instance counter, but shared by multiple runShard goroutines during transition\n- **pendingSamples** (line 87): Prometheus metric used by all shards\n\n## Why This is Intermittent\n\nThe race condition manifests only under specific timing conditions:\n\n1. **Requires timeout during flush:** The `flushDeadline` must expire before all old shards finish sending samples (line 1289)\n2. **Requires concurrent activity:** While `hardShutdown()` is canceling old shards, `start()` must reset the counters before old runShards read them\n3. **Requires samples in flight:** There must be samples in old queues that haven't been sent yet\n4. **Timing-dependent:** The exact interleaving of old shard cleanup and new shard initialization varies based on:\n   - Network latency in remote write destination\n   - System scheduling of goroutines\n   - Load and number of samples being sent\n   - Duration of resharding decision (line 1072)\n\nThe issue is most likely to occur when:\n- Target discovery adds/removes many targets (causes resharding)\n- Remote storage endpoint is slow or temporarily unavailable (triggers timeouts)\n- Prometheus is under moderate load (prevents immediate completion)\n\n## Affected Components\n\n**Primary:**\n- `storage/remote/queue_manager.go` - QueueManager, shards struct, resharding logic\n- Package: `github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage/remote`\n\n**Secondary:**\n- Metrics tracking: `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending`\n- Queue management: Append, FlushAndShutdown, runShard functions\n\n**Impact:**\n- Remote write queue becomes stuck with incorrect metric values\n- Some samples may be silently dropped without proper accounting\n- Resharding can trigger cascading failures in monitoring of remote write capacity\n\n## Recommendation\n\n### Fix Strategy\n\n**Option 1 (Safest): Separate counters per shard instance**\n- Create new `enqueuedSamples` counters in each shard instance rather than resetting\n- Old shards track their dropped samples independently\n- New shards track their enqueued samples independently\n- Prevents cross-contamination during transitions\n\n**Option 2 (Simpler): Snapshot counters before reset**\n- In `stop()`, before calling `hardShutdown()`, capture the current value of `enqueuedSamples`\n- Pass this snapshot to hardshutdown cleanup path\n- Old runShards use the snapshot instead of reading the live atomic value\n- Prevents reads of reset values\n\n**Option 3 (Most robust): Add synchronization between stop and start**\n- Don't call `start()` until all old runShards have completed cleanup\n- Ensure hardshutdown cleanup code runs before metrics are reset\n- Requires stronger synchronization guarantees\n\n### Diagnostic Steps to Confirm\n\n1. **Enable verbose logging** at resharding points:\n   - Log when `hardShutdown()` is called vs when samples were dropped\n   - Log `enqueuedSamples` value before and after `start()`\n\n2. **Monitor these metrics together:**\n   - `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending` (should decrease monotonically)\n   - `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_dropped_total` (should account for drops)\n   - `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_sent_total` (should increase)\n\n3. **Check for correlation:**\n   - When `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending` gets stuck\n   - Check logs for \"Currently resharding, skipping\" messages (line 1076)\n   - Check for timeout messages near `flushDeadline` (default 10s, line 651)\n   - Verify remote endpoint was slow or unavailable at that time\n\n4. **Reproduce with test:**\n   - Create test that triggers resharding while samples are slow to send\n   - Verify metrics are consistent before/after resharding\n   - Check that no samples are lost without being counted in drop metrics\n\n### Immediate Workarounds\n\n1. Increase `flushDeadline` to reduce timeout likelihood\n2. Reduce resharding frequency by adjusting `shardUpdateDuration` (line 1060)\n3. Ensure remote write endpoint has low latency and high availability\n4. Monitor `prometheus_remote_storage_shards` metric for frequent resharding spikes\n"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":12,"cache_creation_input_tokens":4097,"cache_read_input_tokens":58414,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":0,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":4097},"output_tokens":1,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"8bbb7abb-3a6e-4884-b1ad-93bb9c199c14","uuid":"05939bb2-ec36-40c8-8ca3-2c31d4ae6043"}

The issue occurs when the shared sample-counting metrics (`pendingSamples`, `enqueuedSamples`) are reset during shard startup before the hardshutdown cleanup from the previous shard instance completes, causing samples dropped during timeout-triggered hardshutdowns to be incorrectly accounted.\n\n## Root Cause\n\n**File:** `storage/remote/queue_manager.go`\n**Functions:** `reshardLoop()` (line 1184), `stop()` (line 1269), `start()` (line 1237), `runShard()` (line 1491)\n\n**Mechanism:**\n\nThe resharding process has an implicit but unchecked ordering assumption:\n\n```go\n// reshardLoop() at lines 1189-1194\ncase numShards := <-t.reshardChan:\n    t.shards.stop()      // Stops old shards\n    t.shards.start(numShards)  // Starts new shards\n```\n\nDuring `stop()` (lines 1269-1305):\n1. `softShutdown` is closed (prevents new enqueues)\n2. Old queues are told to flush via `FlushAndShutdown()`\n3. If the flush deadline expires, `hardShutdown()` cancels the context\n4. `hardShutdown()` triggers cleanup in old `runShard` goroutines\n\nDuring `start()` (lines 1237-1266):\n1. **Critical reset:** `s.enqueuedSamples.Store(0)` (line 1256)\n2. **Critical reset:** `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Set(0)` (line 1241)\n3. New queues created\n4. New `runShard` goroutines started\n\n**The Race:**\n\n```\nTimeline of the race condition:\n\nt0: Sample S enqueued in old shard\n    → enqueuedSamples = 1, pendingSamples = 1\n\nt1: Resharding triggered, stop() called\n    → softShutdown closed, FlushAndShutdown() for all queues\n\nt2: Flush deadline exceeded\n    → hardShutdown() cancels context\n\nt3: reshardLoop() calls start()\n    → enqueuedSamples.Store(0)   [RESET]\n    → pendingSamples.Set(0)      [RESET]\n    → New shards created\n\nt4: Old runShard sees ctx.Done()\n    → Reads droppedSamples = int(s.enqueuedSamples.Load())\n    → Gets 0 (was reset in t3!)\n    → Decrements pendingSamples -= 0\n    → Sample S is lost but not accounted\n\nt5: New samples enqueued in new shards\n    → enqueuedSamples = 1, pendingSamples = 1\n    → Metrics show pending but don't reflect lost samples\n```\n\nThe old `runShard` goroutine cannot decrement the metrics properly because the counters it depends on were reset by `start()` before it could read them.\n\n## Evidence\n\n### Code References:\n\n1. **Metric Reset in start():**\n   - Line 1241: `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Set(0)`\n   - Line 1256: `s.enqueuedSamples.Store(0)`\n\n2. **Hardshutdown Cleanup Path:**\n   - Lines 1563-1579: When `ctx.Done()` is received\n   - Line 1566: `droppedSamples := int(s.enqueuedSamples.Load())`\n   - Line 1569: `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Sub(float64(droppedSamples))`\n\n3. **Sample Enqueue Path:**\n   - Lines 1313-1333: Enqueue function\n   - Line 1326: `s.qm.metrics.pendingSamples.Inc()`\n   - Line 1327: `s.enqueuedSamples.Inc()`\n\n4. **Resharding Loop:**\n   - Lines 1189-1194: Sequential stop() then start()\n   - Line 1073: Logs \"Remote storage resharding from=4 to=6\"\n\n### Supporting Code Structure:\n\n- **shards struct** (lines 1209-1234): Contains shared atomic counters across all runShard goroutines\n- **enqueuedSamples** (line 1215): Per-shard instance counter, but shared by multiple runShard goroutines during transition\n- **pendingSamples** (line 87): Prometheus metric used by all shards\n\n## Why This is Intermittent\n\nThe race condition manifests only under specific timing conditions:\n\n1. **Requires timeout during flush:** The `flushDeadline` must expire before all old shards finish sending samples (line 1289)\n2. **Requires concurrent activity:** While `hardShutdown()` is canceling old shards, `start()` must reset the counters before old runShards read them\n3. **Requires samples in flight:** There must be samples in old queues that haven't been sent yet\n4. **Timing-dependent:** The exact interleaving of old shard cleanup and new shard initialization varies based on:\n   - Network latency in remote write destination\n   - System scheduling of goroutines\n   - Load and number of samples being sent\n   - Duration of resharding decision (line 1072)\n\nThe issue is most likely to occur when:\n- Target discovery adds/removes many targets (causes resharding)\n- Remote storage endpoint is slow or temporarily unavailable (triggers timeouts)\n- Prometheus is under moderate load (prevents immediate completion)\n\n## Affected Components\n\n**Primary:**\n- `storage/remote/queue_manager.go` - QueueManager, shards struct, resharding logic\n- Package: `github.com/prometheus/prometheus/storage/remote`\n\n**Secondary:**\n- Metrics tracking: `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending`\n- Queue management: Append, FlushAndShutdown, runShard functions\n\n**Impact:**\n- Remote write queue becomes stuck with incorrect metric values\n- Some samples may be silently dropped without proper accounting\n- Resharding can trigger cascading failures in monitoring of remote write capacity\n\n## Recommendation\n\n### Fix Strategy\n\n**Option 1 (Safest): Separate counters per shard instance**\n- Create new `enqueuedSamples` counters in each shard instance rather than resetting\n- Old shards track their dropped samples independently\n- New shards track their enqueued samples independently\n- Prevents cross-contamination during transitions\n\n**Option 2 (Simpler): Snapshot counters before reset**\n- In `stop()`, before calling `hardShutdown()`, capture the current value of `enqueuedSamples`\n- Pass this snapshot to hardshutdown cleanup path\n- Old runShards use the snapshot instead of reading the live atomic value\n- Prevents reads of reset values\n\n**Option 3 (Most robust): Add synchronization between stop and start**\n- Don't call `start()` until all old runShards have completed cleanup\n- Ensure hardshutdown cleanup code runs before metrics are reset\n- Requires stronger synchronization guarantees\n\n### Diagnostic Steps to Confirm\n\n1. **Enable verbose logging** at resharding points:\n   - Log when `hardShutdown()` is called vs when samples were dropped\n   - Log `enqueuedSamples` value before and after `start()`\n\n2. **Monitor these metrics together:**\n   - `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending` (should decrease monotonically)\n   - `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_dropped_total` (should account for drops)\n   - `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_sent_total` (should increase)\n\n3. **Check for correlation:**\n   - When `prometheus_remote_storage_samples_pending` gets stuck\n   - Check logs for \"Currently resharding, skipping\" messages (line 1076)\n   - Check for timeout messages near `flushDeadline` (default 10s, line 651)\n   - Verify remote endpoint was slow or unavailable at that time\n\n4. **Reproduce with test:**\n   - Create test that triggers resharding while samples are slow to send\n   - Verify metrics are consistent before/after resharding\n   - Check that no samples are lost without being counted in drop metrics\n\n### Immediate Workarounds\n\n1. Increase `flushDeadline` to reduce timeout likelihood\n2. Reduce resharding frequency by adjusting `shardUpdateDuration` (line 1060)\n3. Ensure remote write endpoint has low latency and high availability\n4. Monitor `prometheus_remote_storage_shards` metric for frequent resharding spikes\n","structuredPatch":[],"originalFile":null}}
